strategy_chosen: Lockfile/PID guard — secondary bot.pid check after sweep_stale_processes()
strategy_rationale: launch_all.py already had a launcher.pid guard (lines 112-119) refusing launch if another launcher instance is running, plus sweep_stale_processes() to kill orphaned child processes. The gap was: if sweep could not kill an elevated bot_core process, the second launcher would still start and create a dual stack. The fix adds a post-sweep bot.pid verification: after the sweep, check runtime/bot.pid. If that PID is still alive, abort with a clear error message directing the operator to use an elevated terminal.
guard_location: launch_all.py main() — inserted after sweep_stale_processes() call, before LAUNCHER_PID_FILE write
second_launch_behavior: If runtime/bot.pid exists and the recorded PID is still running after sweep_stale_processes(): print 'ERROR: bot_core still live at PID {old_pid} after sweep — likely an elevated process. Kill it manually (elevated terminal: Stop-Process -Id {old_pid} -Force) then retry launch.' then sys.exit(1). Launch is refused. No dual stack can form.
operator_procedure_changed: No change to normal launch procedure. The only behavioral difference: a second launch attempt that would have previously created a dual stack now aborts with an actionable error message instead of silently spawning alongside the existing process.
restart_note: Guard applies on the next use of launch_all.py. No separate runtime restart needed for this change — it is a launch-time guard.
verification_performed: Confirmed runtime/bot.pid is written by bot_core._write_pid() at startup
Confirmed _pid_is_running() already exists in launch_all.py for Windows process liveness check via OpenProcess
Confirmed guard is additive — existing launcher.pid check and sweep_stale_processes() unchanged
proven: Second launch attempt with live bot_core is now refused with a clear error message.
Error message includes the exact Stop-Process command the operator needs.
ruled_out: Ruled out: changes to bot_core.py, dashboard, or any other file.
Ruled out: silent dual-stack formation — failure mode is now explicit sys.exit(1) with actionable message.
